reasoning: Based on climatological averages for Toulouse-Blagnac, the expected daily mean temperature drops significantly from early November into winter. Interpolating 1991-2020 normal mean temperatures gives an expected ~10.8°C for Nov 9, ~9.6°C for Nov 16, ~7.6°C for Dec 9, and ~6.9°C for Feb 7. Utilizing a reasonable standard deviation for daily temperatures in the cooler months (approx. 4.0°C for Nov, 4.5°C for Dec/Feb), the standard deviation of the difference between purely independent days is roughly 5.7°C to 6.0°C. Converting expected differences to z-scores yields probabilities that future dates are warmer than Nov 9 at roughly 42% (Nov 16), 30% (Dec 9), and 26% (Feb 7). With no available live weather forecast predicting large anomalies perfectly aligned with Nov 9, relying directly on derived climatological normals is optimal.
